subject: LU-4712 llite: lock the inode to be migrated

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

To invalidate lustre dentry, setting an INVALID flag is all that's been done. So I don't see the reason to lock inode during migration, could you point out a failure that's caused by this?

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1:

migrate is from ioctl, unlike some other sys_call, and also we need to change the dentry somehow, so we need to lock the inode as other sys_call, IMHO.

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1:

i_mutex has nothing to do with dentry management, if it's needed here, it should be of other reason. BTW you should add a test to reproduce the failure.

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

this will only help when running with racer. IMHO.  I might be wrong about the reason of dentry. But the point is that we do not want other operation even modify the local inode in cache during the migration to avoid un-seeing trouble, and we will also clear the inode from the cache immediately after migration.

Patch Set 2:

Di: I think that you suspect that it is other unlink/rename operations contend with the migration operation, and caused LU-4712 failure by race. If yes, then only taking the i_mutex may be not enough, because after you obtained the i_mutex, the relationship between the name entry and inode may has been been changed.

Patch Set 2:

Not sure I follow you. but why do I care name entry and inode relationship here? The point is to lock the inode like other normal VFS call.
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 3:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 3: Verified-1

Patch Set 3:

> wangdi
> 1:47 AM
> Patch Set 2:
> Not sure I follow you. but why do I care name entry and inode relationship here? The point is to lock the inode like other normal VFS call.

Then I wonder that how your patch can fix the LU-4712 failure? Sorry, I cannot imagine how your patch will affect LU-4712, would please to explain more?

Patch Set 3:

I did not figure out the real reason yet, since this panic only happened once in my local test, I do not have enough information to figure out the reason.  Still keep digging.

This fix is more about "review and fix". I think it makes sense to lock the inode in local cache as we do for other VFS sys call(like rename).  I am not sure what do you against here?  You mean we should not, or not enough?

The patch looks fine, but I still don't understand how it can fix LU-4712, which panic'd on an invalid dentry (possibly a freed one), which this patch just serialise migrate with lookup/rename/.... BTW according to John's test, this issue also happens when migrate is disabled.

Patch Set 5:

I am still investigating that ticket yet. But this patch at least do something right and remove some unnecessary console message.

parents.subject: LU-5242 osd-zfs: umount hang in sanity 133g

commit.subject: LU-4712 llite: lock the inode to be migrated
commit.message:
LU-4712 llite: lock the inode to be migrated

Because the inode and its connected dentries will be cleared
out of the cache after migration, the inode needs to be locked
during the migration.

Signed-off-by: wang di <di.wang@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ibbbb33473de1a67df85ef8930debcf22cd775bcb
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/9689
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
